records.ety: [F. accs, L. accessus, fr. accedere. See Accede.]

defs: A coming to, or near approach; admittance; admission; accessibility; as, to gain access to a prince.
quotes: I did repel his letters, and denied His access to me.

defs: The means, place, or way by which a thing may be approached; passage way; as, the access is by a neck of land.

quotes: During coverture, access of the husband shall be presumed, unless the contrary be shown.

defs: Increase by something added; addition; as, an access of territory. [In this sense accession is more generally used.]
quotes: I, from the influence of thy looks, receive Access in every virtue.

defs: An onset, attack, or fit of disease.
quotes: The first access looked like an apoplexy.

defs: A paroxysm; a fit of passion; an outburst; as, an access of fury.
